meta.title: Group Quote Marketing
meta.catalog: Majesco Distribution & Group Quoting Best-Practice Catalog

steps.output: Prospect logged in CRM; preliminary group size and benefit type recorded
Needs, current insurer, renewal date and decision timeline established

steps.activity: Census data request sent to prospect
steps.output: Meridian Group Census Data Form provided; completion deadline set
Data validated for completeness; missing data followed up within 2 days

steps.activity: Census data submitted to Underwriting and Actuarial
steps.output: Quote request logged with target presentation date; urgency assigned
Experience or community rating by group size; pricing within 5 days for standard groups
Proposal: benefits schedule, rates by tier, 30-day validity, key terms
Final accuracy and marketing-compliance review; approved quote released to agent

branches: Non-standard (>80% loss ratio) → Head of Group (Sec 6.3)

steps.output: Presentation held; proposal submitted; competitor comparison if requested
Revised terms to Underwriting; max 2 revisions without escalation
Signed acceptance or heads of agreement received; accepted in CRM
Case file transferred to SOP-INS-006 for master policy setup

swim_phases.name: Prospect identification & qualification
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 1–4 · source to census
swim_phases.note: Prospects with 10+ lives are qualified and a census data form is collected before quoting can begin.

swim_phases.name: Quote preparation
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 5–8 · pricing to approval
swim_phases.note: Census data is priced by Underwriting/Actuarial and the proposal is compliance-reviewed before release; non-standard groups escalate.

swim_phases.name: Presentation & conversion
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 9–12 · present to handover
swim_phases.note: Proposals are presented and negotiated within revision limits; acceptance hands the case to Group Benefits Administration.

fitgap.summary_line: Overall alignment of SOP-INS-007 against the Majesco distribution & group quoting catalog

items.name: Pipeline & prospect capture
items.detail: CRM prospect logging with size and benefit type aligns with Majesco distribution pipeline management.

items.name: Needs discovery
items.detail: Structured needs discovery capturing incumbent and renewal date aligns with Majesco opportunity qualification.

items.name: Digital census intake
items.detail: Census is collected via a manual form. Majesco supports digital census intake and API import from broker benefits-admin systems, cutting turnaround and transcription error.

items.name: Census validation
items.detail: Completeness is checked manually with 2-day follow-up. Majesco auto-validates census structure and data quality on import.

items.name: Quote request orchestration
items.detail: Quote request logging with urgency and target date aligns with Majesco quote workflow.

items.name: Automated group quoting
items.detail: Even standard groups are manually underwritten. Majesco auto-quotes standard groups via a rating engine with what-if modelling, reserving underwriter time for complex risks.

items.name: Risk-data enrichment
items.detail: Pricing uses census only. Majesco enriches with industry-risk and group claims-history data to refine rating and control anti-selection.

items.name: Proposal generation & validity
items.detail: Formal proposal with tiered rates and 30-day validity aligns with Majesco proposal generation.

items.name: Compliance review of proposal
items.detail: Marketing-compliance review is manual. Majesco applies automated disclosure/compliance checks to proposals before release.

items.name: Digital proposal & e-signature
items.detail: Proposals are presented and accepted via signed letters. Majesco delivers a digital e-proposal with e-signature acceptance and full audit — a conduct and cycle-time gap.

items.name: Negotiation & revision control
items.detail: Revision limits with underwriter re-approval align with Majesco quote-revision governance.

items.name: Win/loss & pipeline analytics
items.detail: Lost business is reviewed monthly by hand. Majesco provides win/loss and conversion analytics with competitor benchmarking for pricing feedback.

items.name: Handover to administration
items.detail: Structured case-file handover to group administration aligns with Majesco sale-to-service transition.

critical_missing.title: Digital census intake & validation
critical_missing.detail: Census is collected on a manual form and checked by hand. The catalog imports digital census via API/upload with automated validation, cutting turnaround and error.

critical_missing.title: Automated group quoting
critical_missing.detail: Standard groups are manually underwritten. The catalog auto-quotes standard groups with what-if modelling, freeing underwriters for complex risks and speeding response.

critical_missing.title: Digital e-proposal & e-signature
critical_missing.detail: Proposals are presented and accepted on paper. The catalog delivers a digital e-proposal with e-signature acceptance and audit — a conduct and cycle-time gap.

critical_missing.title: Win/loss & competitor analytics
critical_missing.detail: Lost business is reviewed manually; the catalog provides win/loss and competitor benchmarking analytics that feed pricing and product competitiveness.

control_bars.name: Pipeline & qualification
control_bars.note: CRM pipeline and needs discovery solid

control_bars.name: Group quoting
control_bars.note: Manual UW even for standard groups

remediations.title: Implement digital census intake & validation
remediations.detail: Accept census via secure upload/API with automated structure and data-quality validation. Effort: 4 weeks.

remediations.title: Automate quoting for standard groups
remediations.detail: Auto-quote standard groups via the rating engine with what-if modelling; route only complex risks to underwriters. Effort: 6 weeks.

remediations.title: Deliver digital e-proposal & e-signature
remediations.detail: Generate a digital e-proposal with e-signature acceptance and full audit trail. Effort: 4 weeks.

remediations.title: Add win/loss & competitor analytics
remediations.detail: Provide conversion, win/loss and competitor-benchmarking dashboards to feed pricing and product. Effort: 3 weeks.

remediations.title: Enrich pricing with risk data & auto compliance checks
remediations.detail: Append industry-risk and group claims-history data and apply automated disclosure/compliance checks to proposals. Effort: 4 weeks.

opt_steps.output: Logged in CRM; size and benefit type recorded
Needs, incumbent and renewal date established
Census imported via secure upload/API; structure and data quality auto-validated
Industry-risk and group claims-history data appended
Standard groups auto-quoted with what-if modelling; complex routed to UW
Experience/community rating for non-standard groups within 5 days
Digital proposal: benefits schedule, tiered rates, 30-day validity
Disclosure and marketing-compliance checks applied before release
Digital proposal presented; competitor comparison if requested
Revised terms via what-if modelling; max 2 revisions without escalation
E-signature acceptance captured with audit; accepted in CRM
Conversion, win/loss and competitor benchmarking dashboards updated

optimised_doc.subtitle: Optimised per Fit-Gap Analysis against the Majesco Distribution & Group Quoting Catalog

blocks: OPTIMISATION SUMMARY — What changed in v2.0

This version incorporates 5 remediation actions from Fit-Gap Report FG-INS-007. Alignment to the Majesco distribution & group quoting catalog increased from 66% to 90%. Critical additions: digital census intake with validation, automated quoting for standard groups, a digital e-proposal with e-signature, win/loss analytics, and risk-data enrichment with automated compliance checks.
NEW STEP (green) — step added to close a gap vs the Majesco catalog
ENHANCED (amber) — existing step updated with additional automation or controls

sections.title: 1. Purpose
blocks: This Standard Operating Procedure defines how group insurance is marketed, quoted and sold to prospective group clients. Version 2.0 incorporates optimisations from fit-gap analysis against the Majesco Distribution & Group Quoting Best-Practice Catalog, achieving 90% alignment (up from 66% in v1.1).

sections.title: 2. Scope
blocks: This procedure applies to group opportunities with 10 or more covered lives across employer-sponsored life, health, disability and accident programmes, and affinity/association groups.
Scope extended: digital census intake and automated quoting now apply to all standard group opportunities.
This procedure does NOT cover individual product campaigns (SOP-INS-001/008), wholesale distribution marketing, or reinsurance treaty placements.

sections.title: 4. Roles and Responsibilities
blocks: Identifies and qualifies prospects; collects census; presents proposals; manages the relationship.
Runs campaigns and collateral; owns win/loss analytics and competitor benchmarking.
Coordinates quoting; prepares e-proposals; manages validity and handover.
Reviews census; sets pricing and conditions; approves quotes for release.

sections.title: 5. Digital Quoting Controls (New Section — v2.0)
blocks: New section. Required by the Majesco catalog. Digital census, automated quoting and e-signature acceptance now govern the group quote lifecycle.

sections.title: 7. Exception Handling
blocks: Reason for loss recorded in CRM with standard 'Lost Business' reason codes.
Win/loss analytics surface pricing or product competitiveness issues automatically.
Agent may request a post-decision debrief to capture prospect feedback.
New Business Specialist notifies the field agent 7 days before expiry.
If an extension is needed, Underwriting re-confirms pricing and reissues with a revised validity date.
Underwriting flags non-standard groups to Compliance and Head of Group Insurance before quoting.
Pricing is loaded with risk margin and exclusions as required.
Head of Group Insurance must approve quotes for groups with loss ratios above 80% over the prior 3 years.

sections.title: 8. Systems and Tools
blocks: Census input, automated pricing, proposal generation
Digital proposal delivery and audited acceptance
Handover to policy setup on acceptance

sections.title: 9. Key Controls and Audit Points
blocks: Controls expanded in v2.0: digital census validation, automated quoting, e-signature acceptance, and automated compliance checks.
Digital census: imported and validated automatically before pricing.
Automated quoting: standard groups auto-quoted; underwriters focus on complex risks.
Compliance check: disclosure and marketing-compliance applied to every proposal.
E-signature: audited digital acceptance replaces paper sign-off.
Risk-data enrichment: industry and claims data improve pricing accuracy.
Revision control: maximum two revisions without escalation, underwriter re-approved.
Immutable audit trail: census, quote, proposal and acceptance events logged.

sections.title: 11. Document Control
blocks: Added non-standard group exception; updated CRM steps; revised conversion KPI targets
OPTIMISED: digital census intake; automated quoting; e-proposal & e-signature; win/loss analytics; risk-data enrichment & compliance checks; catalog alignment 66% → 90%
